openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: Domain package for credit-purchase charge lifecycle: defines Service, Adapter, and Handler interfaces plus all domain types (Charge, Intent, Settlement tagged-union, Status, Realizations). Settlement type is a private-field tagged union (promotional/external/invoice) with full JSON serde. The Handler interface decouples all ledger side-effects from charge service code.

patterns.name: Settlement tagged union via NewSettlement[T]
patterns.description: Settlement is a private-field struct with a SettlementType discriminator t. Always construct via NewSettlement[T] and access via AsInvoiceSettlement/AsExternalSettlement/AsPromotionalSettlement. Never set t, invoice, external, or promotional fields directly.
patterns.example: s := creditpurchase.NewSettlement(creditpurchase.ExternalSettlement{GenericSettlement: creditpurchase.GenericSettlement{Currency: "USD", CostBasis: decimal.NewFromFloat(100)}, InitialStatus: CreatedInitialPaymentSettlementStatus})

patterns.name: Intent.Normalized() before Validate()
patterns.description: Always call intent.Normalized() (which calls meta.Intent.Normalized() and NormalizeOptionalTimestamp, and rounds CreditAmount to currency precision) before Validate(). Skipping normalization allows denormalized timestamps and un-rounded amounts to reach the adapter.
patterns.example:
input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized()
if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Handler abstraction for all ledger interactions
patterns.description: All ledger writes must go through creditpurchase.Handler interface methods: OnPromotionalCreditPurchase, OnCreditPurchaseInitiated, OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized, OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled. Never call ledger APIs directly from charge code.
patterns.example: ref, err := h.handler.OnCreditPurchaseInitiated(ctx, charge)

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError in all Validate()
patterns.description: All Validate() methods collect errors into []error and return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) so nil is returned when errs is empty. Never return raw fmt.Errorf from Validate().
patterns.example: return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...))

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinel errors with HTTP status
patterns.description: Domain-level errors are defined as package-level var using models.NewValidationIssue with an ErrCode constant and commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute, not fmt.Errorf. Use errors.Is to match them upstream.
patterns.example: var ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive, "credit purchase charge is not active", models.WithFieldString("namespace"), models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter (ChargeAdapter + CreditGrantAdapter + ExternalPaymentAdapter + InvoicedPaymentAdapter + TxCreator) and all input types, each with Validate() returning models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new input type must have a Validate() method; namespace must always be checked non-empty.

key_file_guides.role: Charge, ChargeBase, Intent, Settlement type, Realizations, and Status types. Intent.Normalized() truncates timestamps and rounds CreditAmount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditAmount must be positive; settlement currency must match credit currency; EffectiveAt is unsupported and always errors in Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Settlement tagged-union with JSON MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON. Three variants: InvoiceSettlement, ExternalSettlement, PromotionalSettlement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never instantiate Settlement{} directly; always use NewSettlement[T]. GetCostBasis() returns zero for promotional.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface — single abstraction for all ledger side-effects. Promotional uses OnPromotionalCreditPurchase only; paid paths go Initiated -> Authorized -> Settled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler methods must not be called outside the service layer.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface = CreditPurchaseService + ExternalPaymentLifecycle + InvoicePaymentLifecycle. Create returns ChargeWithGatheringLine — the caller must handle GatheringLineToCreate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChargeWithGatheringLine.GatheringLineToCreate may be non-nil; the parent charges.Service must create the gathering line after Create returns.

key_file_guides.role: ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive sentinel ValidationIssue with HTTP 400.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use errors.Is(err, ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive) to detect this condition; do not create new errors for it.

key_file_guides.role: FundedCreditActivity and cursor-based ListFundedCreditActivitiesInput/Result for the credit activity feed.
key_file_guides.watch_for: After and Before cannot both be set; Limit must be >= 1.

key_file_guides.role: Status enum mapping meta.ChargeStatus values for credit-purchase; ToMetaChargeStatus() converts back.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use ToMetaChargeStatus() to convert Status back to meta.ChargeStatus; direct cast bypasses Validate().

anti_patterns: Calling ledger APIs directly instead of routing through creditpurchase.Handler — bypasses credits.enabled=false guard.
Constructing Settlement{} with direct field assignment instead of NewSettlement[T] — leaves discriminator field t empty.
Skipping intent.Normalized() before Validate() — allows denormalized timestamps and un-rounded amounts to reach the adapter.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — breaks error-type detection upstream.
Setting EffectiveAt on Intent — it is explicitly unsupported and will return a validation error.

decisions.decision: Settlement is a tagged union (private fields + discriminator) rather than an interface
decisions.rationale: Enables exhaustive JSON serde and GetCostBasis() on all variants without type assertions at call sites, and prevents partially-constructed Settlement values.

decisions.decision: Handler interface decouples ledger writes from charge service
decisions.rationale: Allows a noop handler when credits.enabled=false without branching inside service code; keeps the ledger abstraction boundary clean.
Gathering line creation must be deferred to the parent charges.Service to fit the billing line lifecycle model without a circular dependency between creditpurchase and billing.

code_examples.scenario: Create a credit purchase charge with invoice settlement

openmeter/billing/charges/lineage.purpose: Domain package for credit realization lineage tracking: defines Service and Adapter interfaces for creating initial lineages, persisting correction segments, and backfilling advance segments. Lineage records link credit realizations to backing transaction groups via append-only Segment rows; mutations use close-then-split semantics rather than in-place updates.

patterns.name: transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step mutations
patterns.description: Service methods that call multiple adapter methods must wrap them in transaction.RunWithNoValue so all steps share the same ctx-bound transaction. Calls outside the closure use a different connection and bypass the transaction.
patterns.example: return transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) error { return s.adapter.CreateLineages(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: Input.Validate() before any business logic
patterns.description: Every service method calls input.Validate() as the first step before any adapter or lock operation. This is separate from the adapter-level validation and must not be skipped.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Lock-then-mutate segment pattern
patterns.description: Adapter lock methods (LockCorrectionLineages, LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill) acquire FOR UPDATE row locks inside an active transaction; must be called before reading segments that will be mutated.
patterns.example: lineages, err := s.adapter.LockCorrectionLineages(ctx, input.Namespace, realizationIDs)

patterns.name: Close-and-split for partial segment mutation
patterns.description: When only part of a segment amount is consumed, close the existing segment (CloseSegment) and create two new segments rather than updating the amount in place. Provides an append-only audit trail.
patterns.example:
s.adapter.CloseSegment(ctx, seg.ID, clock.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond))
s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, CreateSegmentInput{LineageID: seg.LineageID, Amount: consumed, ...})
s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, CreateSegmentInput{LineageID: seg.LineageID, Amount: remaining, ...})

patterns.name: clock.Now() with Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps
patterns.description: All timestamps used for segment closed_at must be truncated to microsecond precision to match Postgres storage precision and avoid equality-check failures. Note: this differs from meta package which truncates to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
patterns.example: closedAt := clock.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Lineage, Segment, ActiveSegmentsByRealizationID, and all input/output types. SortCorrectionPersistSegments and MinDecimal are pure helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BackingTransactionGroupID is required for advance_backfilled and earnings_recognized segments; Segment.Amount must be positive.

key_file_guides.role: Service and Adapter interfaces; all input types with Validate(). BackfillAdvanceLineageSegmentsInput requires positive Amount and non-empty BackingTransactionGroupID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateSegmentInput has complex state-dependent validation rules — source_state and backing_transaction_group_id requirements vary by state.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step adapter calls — creates partial writes when any step fails mid-sequence.
Calling adapter methods outside the transaction.RunWithNoValue closure — the closure's ctx carries the active tx driver; calls outside use a different connection.
Using clock.Now() without Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps — Postgres microsecond precision mismatch causes equality check failures.
Adding Ent query logic directly in service.go — all DB access must go through lineage.Adapter methods.
Removing the GetDriverFromContext guard from lock methods in the adapter — makes FOR UPDATE locks unreliable outside explicit transactions.

decisions.decision: Close-and-split rather than update-in-place for segment mutation
decisions.rationale: Append-only segment history enables auditing and prevents lost-update races under concurrent backfill.

decisions.decision: Lock operations require an active transaction (GetDriverFromContext guard in adapter)
decisions.rationale: FOR UPDATE locks are only meaningful inside a transaction; failing fast prevents silent non-locking reads.

decisions.decision: Package-level LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID alongside the adapter method
decisions.rationale: Allows cross-package callers (creditpurchase service) to load segments without taking a full lineage.Service dependency.

code_examples.scenario: Backfill advance lineage segments inside a transaction

openmeter/billing/charges/meta.purpose: Shared domain primitives for all charge types: ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, ManagedResource, Intent (embedded in every charge intent), Expands, Patch interface (PatchDelete/Extend/Shrink backed by stateless.Trigger constants), and timestamp normalization utilities. Service is a type alias for Adapter — no separate service layer exists.

patterns.name: Patch interface with stateless.Trigger
patterns.description: Every patch type implements Patch: Validate() + Op() PatchType + Trigger() stateless.Trigger + TriggerParams() any. Trigger constants live in triggers.go. New patch types must follow this structure; never define trigger strings inline.

patterns.name: NormalizeTimestamp on all time.Time fields
patterns.description: All timestamps stored in Intent and patches must pass through NormalizeTimestamp (UTC + Truncate(streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration)) before persistence. Call Intent.Normalized() or patch setter methods which call NormalizeTimestamp internally.
patterns.example: i.ServicePeriod = NormalizeClosedPeriod(i.ServicePeriod)

patterns.name: ValidateWith for period bounds checking
patterns.description: PatchExtend and PatchShrink provide ValidateWith(intent Intent) to check new periods against existing intent periods. Always call ValidateWith in addition to Validate() when the existing intent is available.
patterns.example: if err := patch.ValidateWith(charge.Intent); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: ChargeID = models.NamespacedID alias
patterns.description: ChargeID is a type alias of models.NamespacedID; validate with ChargeID.Validate() which delegates to NamespacedID.Validate(). Never access Namespace or ID fields without first calling Validate().
patterns.example: func (i ChargeID) Validate() error { return models.NamespacedID(i).Validate() }

patterns.name: Service = Adapter type alias
patterns.description: meta.Service is a type alias for meta.Adapter — no separate service implementation exists. If complex orchestration is ever needed, remove this alias and add a proper service layer with transaction.Run.
patterns.example: type Service = Adapter

key_file_guides.role: ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, Expand, Expands, ChargeAccessor interface, Charge struct, Charges slice. ChargeAccessor requires GetChargeID(), GetCustomerID(), GetCurrency(), ErrorAttributes().
key_file_guides.watch_for: ExpandNone is nil (not empty slice); every new charge type must implement ChargeAccessor.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Intent struct embedded in all charge intents. Validates ManagedBy, CustomerID, Currency, three periods, TaxConfig, Subscription, UniqueReferenceID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UniqueReferenceID must be nil or non-empty string — empty string fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: PatchDelete + PatchDeletePolicy (CreditRefundPolicy + InvoiceRefundPolicy). RefundAsCreditsDeletePolicy is the recommended default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always set both refund policies; leaving either at zero value fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: NormalizeTimestamp, NormalizeOptionalTimestamp, NormalizeClosedPeriod, Intent.Normalized(). Truncates to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Truncation precision is streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration, NOT time.Microsecond — different from the lineage package.

key_file_guides.role: All stateless.Trigger constants shared across charge state machines: TriggerNext, TriggerPartialInvoiceCreated, TriggerFinalInvoiceCreated, etc.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New triggers must be added here; never define trigger strings inline in state machine configuration files.

key_file_guides.role: PatchExtend and PatchShrink with private fields, typed setters that auto-normalize, and ValidateWith(intent Intent) for bounds checking.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All setters call NormalizeTimestamp internally; use NewPatchExtend/NewPatchShrink constructors which validate before construction.

anti_patterns: Defining charge-type-specific copies of Intent period fields instead of embedding meta.Intent.
Calling NormalizeTimestamp with different truncation precision than streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
Adding complex business logic to the meta package — it is a shared primitives package; logic belongs in type-specific service packages.
Using string literals for trigger constants instead of the vars in triggers.go.
Creating a PatchDelete with only one of the two refund policies set — the other defaults to empty string and fails Validate().

decisions.decision: Service = Adapter type alias with no separate service layer
decisions.rationale: meta is a persistence-thin registry; until orchestration is needed here, the alias avoids premature abstraction.

decisions.decision: Patch interface uses stateless.Trigger rather than a string enum
decisions.rationale: Compile-time type safety with the stateless library; callers pass trigger + params to Machine.FireAndActivate without a string-switch.
ClickHouse window aggregation requires consistent truncation granularity across all charge-related timestamps.

code_examples.scenario: Create and apply a PatchExtend with ValidateWith

openmeter/billing/charges/models.purpose: Structural grouping for shared data-model sub-packages used by all charge types (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase). Sub-packages provide: chargemeta (Ent mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB), creditrealization (allocation/correction ledger records), invoicedusage (accrued usage snapshots), ledgertransaction (GroupReference/TimedGroupReference), and payment (External/Invoiced payment models with Ent mixins). No direct source files live here.

patterns.name: chargemeta.Mixin embedding in all charge Ent schemas
patterns.description: All charge Ent schemas must embed chargemeta.Mixin via Mixin() to get standard charge fields (customer_id, periods, status, currency, managed_by, subscription refs, advance_after). Never re-define these fields per charge type.
patterns.example: func (CreditPurchaseCharge) Mixin() []ent.Mixin { return []ent.Mixin{chargemeta.Mixin{}, ...} }

patterns.name: creditrealization.Mixin + SelfReferenceType
patterns.description: Charge entities that hold credit realizations must use creditrealization.Mixin with SelfReferenceType set to the entity's own type for self-referencing correction edges.
patterns.example: creditrealization.Mixin{SelfReferenceType: entschema.CreditPurchaseRealization{}}

patterns.name: payment.Base hierarchy for new payment variants
patterns.description: Payment entities share a common Base (status, currency, amount, authorized/settled refs) via payment.Mixin. Invoiced adds Immutable line_id/invoice_id. Never embed Base fields directly in a new payment variant.
patterns.example: type ExternalPayment struct { payment.Base; ... }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels in payment/errors.go
patterns.description: All payment domain errors are defined as package-level var ValidationIssue with HTTP status codes, not fmt.Errorf. Use errors.Is to match them.
patterns.example: var ErrAlreadyAuthorized = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeAlreadyAuthorized, "...", commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusConflict))

patterns.name: ledgertransaction.GroupReference nil-safe helpers
patterns.description: Use GetIDOrNull() for SetNillable Ent field methods; call Validate() before accessing TransactionGroupID directly.
patterns.example: creator.SetNillableTransactionGroupID(ref.GetIDOrNull())

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin providing all standard charge columns and generic Create/Update/MapFromDB type-parameterized helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: intent.Normalized() and meta.NormalizeOptionalTimestamp are called at the top of Create/Update — never skip these; SortHint must be unique per batch.

key_file_guides.role: Realization, Realizations, InitialLineageSpec types; allocation/correction duality enforced in CreateInput.Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: SortHint must be non-zero unique values per batch for deterministic correction ordering.

key_file_guides.role: Realizations.Correct() correction planning — must be used instead of manually constructing correction records.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Round amounts via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before calling Correct(); never manually set CorrectsRealizationID.

key_file_guides.role: GroupReference and TimedGroupReference value types. GetIDOrNull() for nil-safe Ent SetNillable methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Constructing TimedGroupReference with a zero Time value — Validate() will reject it.

key_file_guides.role: Invoiced type with Immutable LineID/InvoiceID fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineID and InvoiceID must not be mutated after creation; UpdateInvoicedPayment only for status/transaction group fields.

key_file_guides.role: Domain error sentinels as ValidationIssue with HTTP status codes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use errors.Is; do not create new errors for conditions already covered by these sentinels.

anti_patterns: Defining per-charge-type copies of chargemeta fields instead of embedding chargemeta.Mixin.
Setting SortHint=0 on all realizations in a batch — correction ordering becomes non-deterministic.
Accessing ledgertransaction.TransactionGroupID without calling Validate() first.
Mutating Invoiced.LineID or Invoiced.InvoiceID after creation — these are Immutable Ent fields.
Creating a correction realization without going through Realizations.Correct() — bypasses CorrectsRealizationID assignment and amount validation.

decisions.decision: chargemeta.Mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB instead of per-type boilerplate
decisions.rationale: Three charge types share identical persistence fields; type parameters eliminate duplication while keeping compile-time safety.

decisions.decision: Two-type realization model (allocation + correction) instead of mutable balance rows
decisions.rationale: Append-only model provides a full audit trail for credit reconciliation and avoids lost-update races.

decisions.decision: Separate Invoiced and External payment types sharing a payment.Base
decisions.rationale: Invoiced payments have an immutable invoice/line link not applicable to external payments; separate types prevent accidental mutation.

openmeter/billing/charges/worker.purpose: Organisational parent for charge-advancement workers split by execution model: advance/ for scheduled batch sweeps across all customers with error accumulation and pagination, and asyncadvance/ for single-customer event-driven advancement triggered by a Watermill AdvanceChargesEvent. Both children share the same constraint: all mutations flow through charges.ChargeService, never through Ent adapters directly.

patterns.name: Config struct + constructor validation before use
patterns.description: Each child defines a Config struct with required service fields and validates them in New*() before returning the concrete type. Missing required fields (e.g. ChargesService == nil) return an error immediately.

patterns.name: Service-only access — no direct Ent calls
patterns.description: Both advance.AutoAdvancer and asyncadvance.Handler hold only a charges.ChargeService interface. Ent/adapter packages are never imported or called in these packages.
patterns.example: type AutoAdvancer struct { chargesService charges.ChargeService; logger *slog.Logger }

patterns.name: Error accumulation via errors.Join in batch paths
patterns.description: advance.All collects per-customer errors into []error and returns errors.Join — one failing customer never blocks others. asyncadvance.Handle returns the error directly (single-customer scope).

patterns.name: Pagination via pagination.CollectAll with NewPaginator
patterns.description: advance.ListCustomersToAdvance uses pagination.CollectAll with a pagination.NewPaginator closure so the full customer list is fetched page-by-page without manual cursor management.

patterns.name: Structured logging with slog using context-aware methods
patterns.description: Both packages use log/slog with InfoContext/DebugContext/WarnContext/ErrorContext and named key-value pairs for namespace and customer_id.
patterns.example: h.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "failed to advance charges", slog.String("namespace", event.Namespace), slog.String("customer_id", event.CustomerID))

key_file_guides.role: Batch sweeper: lists all customers with eligible charges via paginated service call, advances each, accumulates errors across customers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time.Now() is captured once at the top of All() and passed to ListCustomersToAdvance — do not capture it inside the paginator closure or per-customer calls.

key_file_guides.role: Single-customer Watermill event handler: constructs customer.CustomerID from event fields and calls AdvanceCharges. Exposes exactly one public method: Handle(ctx, *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns the error from AdvanceCharges — do NOT return nil to suppress retries; Watermill uses the error for retry/dead-letter routing.

anti_patterns: Calling Ent/adapter code directly — all mutations must go through charges.ChargeService.
Returning on first customer error in batch paths — must accumulate with errors.Join.
Introducing context.Background() or context.TODO() — always propagate caller's ctx.
Adding batch/pagination logic to asyncadvance.Handler — that belongs in advance.AutoAdvancer.
Returning nil from asyncadvance.Handle to silently swallow failures — Watermill uses the error for retry/DLQ routing.

decisions.decision: Separate packages for batch (advance) and async (asyncadvance) advance paths
decisions.rationale: Execution models differ: batch sweeps all customers with pagination and error accumulation; async handles a single Watermill event with pass-through error semantics. Mixing them would couple scheduling and event-bus concerns.

decisions.decision: Error accumulation via errors.Join in the batch path instead of early return
decisions.rationale: One failing customer must not prevent others from being advanced; all failures are surfaced together for observability and retry.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing and running the batch sweeper
